info.name: Scalers Operator Console — Demo
info.description: Live demo collection for the Scalers operator console backend (GraphQL + SSE over Postgres). Select the 'Scalers Local' environment first. Queries are read-only; the approveAction mutation FIRES THE REAL CONNECTOR (real send). The SSE request streams every internal step live.

item.name: Overview (KPIs + attention + recent runs + health + feed)

request.description: The Overview screen composite. Shows the autonomy %, review-queue count, attention items, recent runs, system health, and a live-feed preview.
The escalated actions awaiting human approval. Each carries the per-dimension jury (voice/safety/appr), pooled confidence + threshold, gate results, and the idempotency key.

item.name: Action detail (one action, full jury card)

request.description: A single action by id — the detail pane of the review queue.

item.name: Activity (executed actions: exec-trace + per-judge jury + links)

request.description: Executed actions (status=sent). Shows the EXECUTION TRACE (spans: llm draft -> jury votes -> gates -> route decision), the 4 cross-family judges with reasoning, and the LINKS to the real post/email.
LangGraph/Temporal run history. `events` is the RUN HISTORY: each step expands to a nested span tree (kind/title/ms/detail) — the internal trace of each node + cell.
A single run by id with its full event/span trajectory.
The decision/event feed (worker, text, chip, severity).
Deliverability + quota health (gmail warmup, IG publish quota, checkpoint status).

WARNING: this is the human-authorized side effect. For a GMAIL action it sends a REAL email; for IG/FB it makes a real Graph call. The action flips to status=sent with a real deep_link (or fails with the real provider error — never a fake success).
Operator declines the action. No send happens; status=rejected.
Edit the draft in place before approving (benign, no send).

item.name: Live SSE stream  [watch every internal step in real time]

request.description: Server-Sent Events stream. In Postman, click Send — the response stays open and streams the canonical events (feed.event, action.created, action.updated, run.updated, kpi.updated, health.updated, toast) as they happen, so you can show the internal steps live. (Postman v10+ renders text/event-stream as an Event Stream.)
